skill.name: add-core-function
skill.description: Add new core business logic functions to Catalyst-Relay using consistent TypeScript file, import, return, and export conventions.
skill.summary: Guides contributors through adding Catalyst-Relay core functions with project-specific TypeScript patterns.
skill.author: Artisan-Edge

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backticks, relative TypeScript import examples, and the word description as security patterns. Manual review found no executable shell commands, no path traversal behavior, no weak cryptography, no network exfiltration, and no prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution are false positives. The flagged lines are Markdown inline code, fenced directory examples, or TypeScript examples, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pattern appears only in Markdown formatting and TypeScript documentation blocks. No command execution API or executable script is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Relative Import Paths Misidentified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: Static path traversal findings are false positives. The flagged paths are TypeScript import examples and project layout guidance for local source files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relative paths are static import references such as shared types and helpers. I found no file read, file write, archive extraction, or user-controlled filesystem path handling.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Misidentified
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings at lines 3 and 66 are false positives. They match ordinary description text, not cryptographic algorithms or security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain the word description in metadata and an interface field. No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or cryptographic operation is present.

content.value_statement: Core changes need consistent files, imports, returns, and exports. This skill guides Catalyst-Relay business logic additions with typed patterns and checklists.
actual_capabilities: Defines the one-function-per-file structure under src/core/{domain}/{function}.ts.
Shows the required import hierarchy for types, helpers, subfolders, and barrel exports.
Provides a TypeScript async function pattern using AsyncResult tuples.
Explains when public core functions should be exported from index.ts.
Gives a checklist for helper placement, circular dependency avoidance, and type checking.
limitations: It is specific to Catalyst-Relay core layout and naming conventions.
It does not provide complete business implementations for every function.
It does not replace tests, project review, or type checking.
It assumes TypeScript and existing Catalyst-Relay utility patterns.

use_cases.title: Add an ADT Operation
use_cases.description: Create a new discovery, preview, read, or create function that follows Catalyst-Relay core conventions.

use_cases.title: Standardize Public Exports
use_cases.description: Place a new core function in the right folder and expose it through the correct barrel export.

use_cases.title: Onboard a Contributor
use_cases.description: Teach a contributor how core files, helpers, return types, and import paths should be organized.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Core Function
prompt_templates.prompt: Use add-core-function to add a pure core function for [domain]. Name it [function] and explain each file change.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple new function.

prompt_templates.title: Add an ADT Discovery Operation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use add-core-function to design a new ADT discovery operation named [operation]. Include file placement, return type, helper usage, and export guidance.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a project-specific operation.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Core Function Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use add-core-function to review this implementation plan: [plan]. Check file location, imports, AsyncResult use, helper placement, and barrel exports.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Core API Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use add-core-function to refactor these proposed core API changes: [changes]. Identify circular dependency risks, public API boundaries, and missing typecheck steps.

output_examples.input: Add a getPackages discovery operation for ADT.
output: Recommended file placement under the ADT discovery folder.
Public package type definition near the function.
AsyncResult return convention with requestor error handling.
Barrel export guidance for the ADT public API.

output_examples.input: Review my plan for a new create operation.
output: Checks that the function belongs in the CRAUD folder.
Flags helper logic that should move into helpers.ts.
Confirms shared types should come from types.ts.
Reminds the user to run the project typecheck.

best_practices: Keep one public core function per file unless the project pattern already differs.
Use AsyncResult tuples for async functions that can fail.
Export only public API functions from index.ts and keep internal helpers local.
anti_patterns: Do not place multiple unrelated core functions in one file.
Do not create circular imports between types, helpers, subfolders, and index files.
Do not export internal helper functions through the public barrel file.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create Catalyst-Relay core business logic functions with consistent file structure, imports, return types, and exports.

faq.question: Does it write complete business logic automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides structure and patterns, but the user must supply the exact business behavior.

faq.question: Which return style does it recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends Go-style AsyncResult tuples for async functions that may return data or errors.

faq.question: Where should shared core types go?
faq.answer: Shared types should go in types.ts when they are used by multiple core files.

faq.question: When should a function be added to index.ts?
faq.answer: Add it to index.ts only when it is part of the public library API.

faq.question: Does the skill require command execution?
faq.answer: No. It recommends running the project typecheck, but it does not contain executable commands or scripts.
